items.title: Canberra paramedics become patients every couple of days

children.content: ACT Ambulance Service recorded more than 190 occupational violence incidents last financial year.

image.altText: Mr Dutton smiles in his uniform, standing in front of the logo for the ACT Emergency Services Agency.

items.title: ADFA cadet facing 79 extra charges after hidden camera found in bathroom

children.content: Police allege Tain Stephens was living at the Australian Defence Force Academy in Canberra when he hid a camera inside a soap dispenser in a female bathroom to capture intimate recordings of women.

image.altText: A man with short hair wearing a t-shirt walks out of a court building.

items.title: Year 11 and 12 students should be forced to study maths, review says

children.content: A committee has found students in the ACT should study maths through to the end of year 12 to ensure they have better long-term outcomes, arguing a lack of maths understanding can exacerbate social inequality.

gallery.altText: A generic photo of a student writing on a pad.

thumbnail.altText: A generic photo of a student writing on a pad.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: ACT's school language education plan 'not fit for purpose', committee finds

children.content: The ACT government's blueprint to deliver language education in Canberra public schools is "not fit for purpose", a parliamentary committee finds, prompting calls for the government to once again deliver a centralised ATAR-level language learning plan.

image.altText: Students look at a whiteboard with a mixture of English and Japanese writing on it, as a teacher stands nearby.

items.title: 'Terrifying' roadside assistance ordeal sparks national change

children.content: A woman's campaign for change after her own frightening experience being broken down outside Canberra will see NRMA roadside assistance contractors required to provide a code to customers to verify their identity.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder-length brown hair and glasses stands in front of a road with a phone in her hand.

items.title: 'Never trust a label': Mystery violin willed to Canberra Youth Orchestra

children.content: A violin gifted to not-for-profit organisation Music for Canberra bears the hallmarks of early 18th century European style. But one expert says only forensic testing can prove its true origins.

gallery.altText: A man with short black hair plays the violin.

thumbnail.altText: A man with short black hair plays the violin.

items.title: This nursery owner doesn't use Telstra, but the outage still cost her dearly

children.content: The economic cost of the latest Telstra network outage could run into hundreds of millions of dollars, an expert estimates.

articles.title: Telstra solves 'software defect' after hundreds of failed Triple Zero calls

children.content: Telstra has implemented a fresh "solution" to resolve a secondary software defect that left hundreds of customers unable to make Triple Zero calls during a nationwide network outage.

image.altText: A blurred woman with dark hair and a white top looking at a mobile phone in front of a clear Telstra orange and white logo.

gallery.altText: A woman in a beret stands surrounded by greenery in her nursery, staring at the camera

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a beret stands surrounded by greenery in her nursery, staring at the camera

items.title: 'Bad mistake': ABC's editorial director questioned over inaccurate report

children.content: The ABC and SBS were called to give evidence at the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion, with the inquiry hearing that a number of complaints had been received about the public broadcasters' coverage of the conflict in the Middle East.

image.altText: Mr Fang wearing a suit and blue tie while walking down a street path

items.title: 'Pretty cool': Trump announces win for Ukraine amid fiery NATO meeting

children.content: Donald Trump says his country will probably continue to attack Iran and says Ukraine will be given permission to manufacture much-needed Patriot missiles as it fights Russia's full-scale invasion.

image.altText: Two men in suits sitting and speaking.

items.title: Neil the seal may have left Tasmania, wildlife experts say

children.content: Authorities in Tasmania say it appears Neil the seal may have left his resting spot at a Hobart beach and returned to sea.

gallery.altText: A five-year-old elephant seal known as Neil the Seal laying on a road at Seven Mile Beach, Tasmania.
An elephant seal known as Neil the Seal lies on a beach with people watching him from a safe distance.
A five-year-old elephant seal known as Neil the seal, playing with a traffic cone.

thumbnail.altText: A five-year-old elephant seal known as Neil the Seal laying on a road at Seven Mile Beach, Tasmania.
An elephant seal known as Neil the Seal lies on a beach with people watching him from a safe distance.
A five-year-old elephant seal known as Neil the seal, playing with a traffic cone.

items.title: 'Cannot remain silent': Egypt demands FIFA investigate World Cup referees

children.content: Egypt remains incensed at French referee Francois Letexier after his performance in the Round of 16 clash against Argentina at the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: Mohamed Salah and the Egypt team argue with a referee

items.title: NSW Police officer taken into custody after sexually assaulting then-wife

children.content: A NSW Police officer has been taken into custody after his bail was revoked by a judge, two days after he was found guilty of choking and sexually assaulting his then-wife.

image.altText: Generic image of NSW Police officer.

items.title: State of Origin decider breaks broadcast record

children.content: An unprecedented number of viewers tuned into the State of Origin decider, in which NSW stunned Queensland to win the series.

gallery.altText: A NSW State of Origin captain stands with his team on the dais as confetti falls, holding the shield after a series win.

thumbnail.altText: A NSW State of Origin captain stands with his team on the dais as confetti falls, holding the shield after a series win.

items.title: Court told former Wagga Wagga MP was 'kept in dark' over visa scheme

children.content: Daryl Maguire's barrister has told a jury it was "unbelievable" to think the ex NSW MP would recruit people in his electorate to take part in an allegedly fraudulent visa scheme.

image.altText: A man in a dark jacket, white shirt and tie leaves a building

items.title: Search for crew continues after Boeing cargo plane crash

children.content: Rescue crews battle rough sea conditions to recover parts of a Boeing cargo plane that crashed into the Arabian Sea. Five crew members remain missing.

image.altText: Rescuers wearing safety gear unload debris from a small boat

items.title: This Place: Koala Brother, Yarriabini

children.content: Uncle Micklo Jarrett is a Gumbaynggirr man, sharing a dreamtime story about how Yarriabini mountain, on the mid north coast of New South Wales, got its name.
As part of NAIDOC Week the ABC is looking at Indigenous peoples' connection to country, and what makes it so special.
NAIDOC Week is marking 50 years of Deadly and runs from July 5 until July 12.

image.altText: Man sits on a hill at the beach describing a dreamtime story

gallery.altText: Man sits on a hill at the beach describing a dreamtime story

thumbnail.altText: Man sits on a hill at the beach describing a dreamtime story

items.title: The grief of childlessness will always be with me

children.content: The grief of losing the chance to have baby is something I expect to carry forever. But I'm becoming more comfortable with the unconventional life I'm building with my partner April.

image.altText: April and Sally smiling at each other on the couch

items.title: TV catchphrase quiz: Guess the show from the famous quote

children.content: Now for the story of 20 famous TV quotes, and the one quiz that had no choice but to keep them all together.

image.altText: Jessica Walter and Jason Bateman in a scene from Arrested Development.

items.title: 'Important' Aboriginal language statement released amid 'abysmal' loss

children.content: Aboriginal language custodians release a road map for the survival and revival of Northern Territory Indigenous languages.

articles.title: The 'walking archive' helping First Nations people discover family history

children.content: For 28 years, Ali Abdullah-Highfold has helped thousands of Indigenous people find familial connections through his work at the South Australian Museum.

image.altText: A man looks at the camera while books are on a table in front of him

articles.title: Five podcasts we're listening to this NAIDOC Week

children.content: Some of our favourite Indigenous-led podcasts this week and every week include interviews with AFL greats, a dive into Aboriginal astronomy, and conversations with a renowned artist who loves Dolly Parton.

image.altText: podcast image NAIDOC week

items.title: Water in the air an 'untapped water resource' until now

children.content: Can water be made from air in WA's Wheatbelt? Researchers are putting an invention to the test.

image.altText: A woman stands with solar panels behind her, she is looking up.

items.title: Swimming Australia fights 'scary' fake news in world-first initiative

children.content: In a world-first initiative, Swimming Australia is taking a proactive approach to deal with clickbait and fake news headlines about its athletes, including Olympic champion Mollie O'Callaghan, who has been targeted in the past.

image.altText: Mollie O'Callaghan in the water after the 200m freestyle.

items.title: Gout Gout ruled out of World Under-20 athletics titles after training injury

children.content: Australian athletics star Gout Gout is ruled out of this year's World Under-20 titles in Oregon after a training injury in Brisbane.

gallery.altText: Gout Gout competing at the Oslo Diamond League meet.

thumbnail.altText: Gout Gout competing at the Oslo Diamond League meet.

items.title: Growers push to overturn ban on ancient 'superfood'

children.content: Farmers are appealing against a federal ban on the sale of
Moringa oleifera
as a food after a regulatory body determined there was insufficient evidence to prove its safety.

image.altText: A man holding up two pots in a greenhouse surrounded by small trees in pots.

items.title: Man, 34, charged with murder over death of girl, 17

children.content: Days after a 17-year-old girl was found dead inside a home in the remote community of Galiwin'ku, police have charged a 34-year-old man following an autopsy.

image.altText: An aerial view of the township of Galiwin'ku on Elcho Island.

items.title: The key ingredient keeping the snow season alive

children.content: Data shows snow depth has been declining in Australia since the 1950s, with resorts turning to snowmaking to guarantee a strong ski season.

items.title: 'Going to make it double': Boxer's jokes about domestic violence condemned

children.content: A leading violence prevention organisation says an Australian boxer's joke about domestic violence is unacceptable.

image.altText: Australian boxer Benjamin Hussain deleted a video joking about domestic violence

items.title: Trainee surgeon found with 4,500 videos from hospital bathrooms pleads guilty

children.content: Ryan Cho has pleaded guilty to 13 charges, including stalking, producing an intimate image and installing an optical surveillance device.

image.altText: Ryan Cho wears sunglasses and a surgical mask as he walks down the street. A reporter holds a microphone towards him.

items.title: Streets reopen around New York high-rise after buckled beams stabilised

children.content: A Midtown Manhattan high-rise being converted into apartments has been stabilised after buckling support columns triggered evacuations and fears of a collapse.

gallery.altText: Three people in hard hats and hi-vis vests are seen through a window, inspecting a support beam.

thumbnail.altText: Three people in hard hats and hi-vis vests are seen through a window, inspecting a support beam.

items.title: Developer plans to demolish and rebuild Brisbane's Wintergarden mall

children.content: Developer IFM has submitted plans to demolish the "fragmented" interior of a Queen Street Mall shopping centre and replace it with two levels of shops.

image.altText: The exterior of a shopping centre.

items.title: Boy charged with extremist offence 'wanted to kill people for months'

children.content: According to court documents, the 13-year-old told police he had planned mass murder for many months, had been inspired by other school shooters and massacres.

image.altText: A BP service station pictured at night.

items.title: 'Profound injustice': Last woman executed in UK pardoned by king

children.content: The case of Ruth Ellis has gripped Britain for decades and inspired the 1985 film Dance with a Stranger.

gallery.altText: A black and white photo of a blonde woman holding a glass of wine.

thumbnail.altText: A black and white photo of a blonde woman holding a glass of wine.

items.title: FIFA boss referred to Olympics ethics board over Trump World Cup call

children.content: Gianni Infantino's close ties with Donald Trump continue to land him in hot water after the US President's World Cup intervention.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Gianni Infantino standing with each other

items.title: Emmy nominations 2026 revealed, with The Pitt and Hacks topping list

children.content: The Pitt takes the lead as the most nominated series for the 78th annual Primetime Emmy Awards, with Hacks in second place.

image.altText: THE PITT

items.title: Jake answers the call and saddles up for ride of a lifetime

children.content: "A mate rang me up and said, 'Do you want to have a go?'" That's all it took to get Jake Callagan in the saddle for the Jundah Camel races. The novice camel jockey took to it well, with a swag of blue, red and green ribbons to take home.
